TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr_farming_Muu
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: sua makasin ia tecuku una kari poisua.
S.PHON: sua makasin ia teʦuku una kar~ɾi poisua.
S.FORM: sua makasin ia tecuku una kari poisua.
S.PHON: sua makasin ia teʦuku una kar~ɾi poisua.
S.TRANSL: 現在,我有話要說.
Now, I have something to say.

S.FORM: ikita nimʉ nimʉmʉkʉmita miana.
S.PHON: ikita nimɨ nimɨmɨkɨmita miana.
S.FORM: ikita nimʉ nimʉmʉkʉmita miana.
S.PHON: ikita nimɨ nimɨmɨkɨmita miana.
S.TRANSL: 我們以前種東西.
We planted crops in the past.

S.FORM: mehua ia matirupangcu ikita ʉ taiuan isi aceicu ngʉco lipʉn.
S.PHON: mehua ia matir~ɾupaŋʦu ikita ɨ taiuan isi aʦeiʦu ŋɨʦo *ipɨn.
S.FORM: mehua ia matirupangcu ikita ʉ taiuan isi aceicu ngʉco lipʉn.
S.PHON: mehua ia matir~ɾupaŋʦu ikita ɨ taiuan isi aʦeiʦu ŋɨʦo *ipɨn.
S.TRANSL: 我們在台灣,完成了,日本就走了.
We were in Taiwan. It was finished. Japan had gone.

S.FORM: makasua mosaci kia na rʉmʉra mavici kakamarʉ nʉman pʉrʉcʉng mata nononoman tia avicin kavang.
S.PHON: makasua mosaʦi kia na r~ɾɨmɨr~ɾa maviʦi kakamar~ɾɨ nɨman pɨr~ɾɨʦɨŋ mata nononoman tia aviʦin kavaŋ.
S.FORM: makasua mosaci kia na rʉmʉra mavici kakamarʉ nʉman pʉrʉcʉng mata nononoman tia avicin kavang.
S.PHON: makasua mosaʦi kia na r~ɾɨmɨr~ɾa maviʦi kakamar~ɾɨ nɨman pɨr~ɾɨʦɨŋ mata nononoman tia aviʦin kavaŋ.
S.TRANSL: 去山上那邊帶鐮刀帶番刀帶東西,都要帶.
Then, to go to the mountains, we would have to bring sickles, knives, chops etc.

S.FORM: makasuacu tumatakʉtakʉci kia capuku tia ka'umo'uma.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu tumatakɨtakɨʦi kia ʦapuku tia kaʔumoʔuma.
S.FORM: makasuacu tumatakʉtakʉci kia capuku tia ka'umo'uma.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu tumatakɨtakɨʦi kia ʦapuku tia kaʔumoʔuma.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣,我們砍芒草做田.
Then, we cut miscanthus to make fields.

S.FORM: nuu ninamaranci ia mosa ci 'inia mavici tarimu tuku kamarʉ mata numan.
S.PHON: nuu ninamar~ɾanʦi ia mosa ʦi ʔinia maviʦi tar~ɾimu tuku kamar~ɾɨ mata numan.
S.FORM: nuu ninamaranci ia mosa ci 'inia mavici tarimu tuku kamarʉ mata numan.
S.PHON: nuu ninamar~ɾanʦi ia mosa ʦi ʔinia maviʦi tar~ɾimu tuku kamar~ɾɨ mata numan.
S.TRANSL: 當它們被燒完了以後,我們就去那邊,帶著大小鋤頭、鐮刀和刀子.
After the miscanthus had been burned out, we went there with hoes, sickles, and knives.

S.FORM: makasuaci ia sua nguco ra'ʉvʉmaku ia mʉmʉkʉcu tammi tanʉkʉ tʉvʉsʉ mata tangtang.
S.PHON: makasuaʦi ia sua ŋuʦo r~ɾaʔɨvɨmaku ia mɨmɨkɨʦu tammi tanɨkɨ tɨvɨsɨ mata taŋtaŋ.
S.FORM: makasuaci ia sua nguco ra'ʉvʉmaku ia mʉmʉkʉcu tammi tanʉkʉ tʉvʉsʉ mata tangtang.
S.PHON: makasuaʦi ia sua ŋuʦo r~ɾaʔɨvɨmaku ia mɨmɨkɨʦu tammi tanɨkɨ tɨvɨsɨ mata taŋtaŋ.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣,我的太太呢, 種地瓜、芋頭、甘蔗和南瓜.
Then, my wife started planting sweet potatoes, taros, sugar canes, and pumpkins.

S.FORM: kavang nimʉmʉkʉ kavang mehua nenen vanaita makasua? mumumuku ngʉco sua nononoman manasi 'an kia tia mooravu.
S.PHON: kavaŋ nimɨmɨkɨ kavaŋ mehua nenen vanaita makasua? mumumuku ŋɨʦo sua nononoman manasi ʔan kia tia moor~ɾavu.
S.FORM: kavang nimʉmʉkʉ kavang mehua nenen vanaita makasua? mumumuku ngʉco sua nononoman manasi 'an kia tia mooravu.
S.PHON: kavaŋ nimɨmɨkɨ kavaŋ mehua nenen vanaita makasua? mumumuku ŋɨʦo sua nononoman manasi ʔan kia tia moor~ɾavu.
S.TRANSL: 為什麼我們要這樣種東西,因為以後不會餓.
Why we planted things this way? It is because we wouldn't get hungry.

S.FORM: makasuacu mumukucu noo acanci vuan urucincu vuan teencu kʉnʉʉn.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu mumukuʦu noo aʦanʦi vuan ur~ɾuʦinʦu vuan teenʦu kɨnɨɨn.
S.FORM: makasuacu mumukucu noo acanci vuan urucincu vuan teencu kʉnʉʉn.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu mumukuʦu noo aʦanʦi vuan ur~ɾuʦinʦu vuan teenʦu kɨnɨɨn.
S.TRANSL: 種下去過一個月兩個月就可以吃了.
After one to two months, we could eat them.

S.FORM: mesua tammi ia tatia pa macacasʉ pa.
S.PHON: mesua tammi ia tatia pa maʦaʦasɨ pa.
S.FORM: mesua tammi ia tatia pa macacasʉ pa.
S.PHON: mesua tammi ia tatia pa maʦaʦasɨ pa.
S.TRANSL: 那時候地瓜會很大很長.
At that time, sweet potatoes will become big and long.

S.FORM: mamanʉng pa akia tʉnua.
S.PHON: mamanɨŋ pa akia tɨnua.
S.FORM: mamanʉng pa akia tʉnua.
S.PHON: mamanɨŋ pa akia tɨnua.
S.TRANSL: 那時候地瓜還很好,沒有蟲蟲.
At that time, sweet potatoes were well-planted. There were no worms.

S.FORM: mesua ia vo'ura pa 'ʉnai.
S.PHON: mesua ia voʔur~ɾa pa ʔɨnai.
S.FORM: mesua ia vo'ura pa 'ʉnai.
S.PHON: mesua ia voʔur~ɾa pa ʔɨnai.
S.TRANSL: 那時候田還新的.
At that time, fields were still new.

S.FORM: noo akeen ngʉco sua akeen tia kʉna manu ia 'una seritung una navatʉ una tʉvʉsʉ.
S.PHON: noo akeen ŋɨʦo sua akeen tia kɨna manu ia ʔuna ser~ɾituŋ una navatɨ una tɨvɨsɨ.
S.FORM: noo akeen ngʉco sua akeen tia kʉna manu ia 'una seritung una navatʉ una tʉvʉsʉ.
S.PHON: noo akeen ŋɨʦo sua akeen tia kɨna manu ia ʔuna ser~ɾituŋ una navatɨ una tɨvɨsɨ.
S.TRANSL: 如果小朋友沒有吃東西的時候,還好有木瓜、芭樂,和甘蔗.
If kids had nothing to eat, thankfully there were papayas, guavas, and sugarcane.

S.FORM: maacancangarʉ mamanu noo ivatuci mamarurang muatanasʉ nimavici navatʉ tʉvʉsʉ siaritung.
S.PHON: maaʦanʦaŋar~ɾɨ mamanu noo ivatuʦi mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ muatanasɨ nimaviʦi navatɨ tɨvɨsɨ siar~ɾituŋ.
S.FORM: maacancangarʉ mamanu noo ivatuci mamarurang muatanasʉ nimavici navatʉ tʉvʉsʉ siaritung.
S.PHON: maaʦanʦaŋar~ɾɨ mamanu noo ivatuʦi mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ muatanasɨ nimaviʦi navatɨ tɨvɨsɨ siar~ɾituŋ.
S.TRANSL: 父母回家的時候帶了芭樂,甘蔗,木瓜,小孩子都很開心快樂.
Parents went home with guava, sugarcane, and papayas. That made kids very happy.

S.FORM: makasuacu po'ocipin ngʉco rʉ'ʉvʉmaku tammi mata tanʉkʉ.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu poʔoʦipin ŋɨʦo r~ɾɨʔɨvɨmaku tammi mata tanɨkɨ.
S.FORM: makasuacu po'ocipin ngʉco rʉ'ʉvʉmaku tammi mata tanʉkʉ.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu poʔoʦipin ŋɨʦo r~ɾɨʔɨvɨmaku tammi mata tanɨkɨ.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣,我的太太煮地瓜和芋頭.
My wife cooked sweet potatoes and taros.

S.FORM: noo macuruci ia aricaancikee na na toomarʉ masii maraa mara mataturicang.
S.PHON: noo maʦur~ɾuʦi ia ar~ɾiʦaanʦikee na na toomar~ɾɨ masii mar~ɾaa mar~ɾa matatur~ɾiʦaŋ.
S.FORM: noo macuruci ia aricaancikee na na toomarʉ masii maraa mara mataturicang.
S.PHON: noo maʦur~ɾuʦi ia ar~ɾiʦaanʦikee na na toomar~ɾɨ masii mar~ɾaa mar~ɾa matatur~ɾiʦaŋ.

TRANSL.notes: 她撈起(地瓜)放在簸箕裡,使比較涼.

S.TRANSL: 她撈起 放在簸箕裡,使比較涼.
When they were well-cooked, she scooped up and put them on a scoop to cool them.

S.FORM: nuu matarang mataturicangci ia monci nia mamarurang xx apakʉn maman mamanuin.
S.PHON: nuu matar~ɾaŋ matatur~ɾiʦaŋʦi ia monʦi nia mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ ** apakɨn maman mamanuin.
S.FORM: nuu matarang mataturicangci ia monci nia mamarurang xx apakʉn maman mamanuin.
S.PHON: nuu matar~ɾaŋ matatur~ɾiʦaŋʦi ia monʦi nia mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ ** apakɨn maman mamanuin.
S.TRANSL: 當涼了以後,餵養她的小孩.
After the food were cooled, the parent fed her kids.

S.FORM: mehua ia cucuru miarinasʉkia akia nupasʉ suai mehua ia.
S.PHON: mehua ia ʦuʦur~ɾu miar~ɾinasɨkia akia nupasɨ suai mehua ia.
S.FORM: mehua ia cucuru miarinasʉkia akia nupasʉ suai mehua ia.
S.PHON: mehua ia ʦuʦur~ɾu miar~ɾinasɨkia akia nupasɨ suai mehua ia.
S.TRANSL: 那時,我非常困苦,沒有錢財.
At that time, my life was really hard. I didn't have much money.

S.FORM: nakai tamu'iarʉ mamarurang umʉʉmʉkʉ sua ngʉco ta kʉna mamanu ia kan ta merinasʉ mamanmita.
S.PHON: nakai tamuʔiar~ɾɨ mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ umɨɨmɨkɨ sua ŋɨʦo ta kɨna mamanu ia kan ta mer~ɾinasɨ mamanmita.
S.FORM: nakai tamu'iarʉ mamarurang umʉʉmʉkʉ sua ngʉco ta kʉna mamanu ia kan ta merinasʉ mamanmita.
S.PHON: nakai tamuʔiar~ɾɨ mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ umɨɨmɨkɨ sua ŋɨʦo ta kɨna mamanu ia kan ta mer~ɾinasɨ mamanmita.

TRANSL.notes: 但是若長輩(父母親)很勤勞地的種植,要給小孩吃的話,我們的小孩就不會困苦了

S.TRANSL: 但是若長輩 很勤勞地的種植,要給小孩吃的話,我們的小孩就不會困苦了
But if parents worked diligently to care their children, children would not have hard lives.

S.FORM: makasuacu unacu arane na cau karanana nimavici pusiam.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu unaʦu ar~ɾane na ʦau kar~ɾanana nimaviʦi pusiam.
S.FORM: makasuacu unacu arane na cau karanana nimavici pusiam.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu unaʦu ar~ɾane na ʦau kar~ɾanana nimaviʦi pusiam.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣,有了從外地來的人攜帶了稻米.
Then, some people outside world brought rice in.

S.FORM: sua pusiam ihua ia tavarʉ'ʉn kumakare na kariupupang na 'ʉne.
S.PHON: sua pusiam ihua ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨn kumakar~ɾe na kar~ɾiupupaŋ na ʔɨne.
S.FORM: sua pusiam ihua ia tavarʉ'ʉn kumakare na kariupupang na 'ʉne.
S.PHON: sua pusiam ihua ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨn kumakar~ɾe na kar~ɾiupupaŋ na ʔɨne.
S.TRANSL: 這個稻米可以種植在旱地
Rice could be planted in dry land.

S.FORM: tavarʉ'ʉn cumancu.
S.PHON: tavar~ɾɨʔɨn ʦumanʦu.
S.FORM: tavarʉ'ʉn cumancu.
S.PHON: tavar~ɾɨʔɨn ʦumanʦu.
S.TRANSL: 可以被插秧.
Rice seedlings can be transplanted.

S.FORM: aranecu mehua kumakare pusiam nakai aka miana itumuru tapuroo.
S.PHON: ar~ɾaneʦu mehua kumakar~ɾe pusiam nakai aka miana itumur~ɾu tapur~ɾoo.
S.FORM: aranecu mehua kumakare pusiam nakai aka miana itumuru tapuroo.
S.PHON: ar~ɾaneʦu mehua kumakar~ɾe pusiam nakai aka miana itumur~ɾu tapur~ɾoo.
S.TRANSL: 從那個時候開始,在旱地種稻米,但是以前很麻煩,有很多麻雀.
Since then, we planted rice on dry lands. However, it was bad that there were a lot of sparrows.

S.FORM: nukai iname ia tia kovangvangʉn ma tapuroo sua ngʉco pusiam.
S.PHON: nukai iname ia tia kovaŋvaŋɨn ma tapur~ɾoo sua ŋɨʦo pusiam.
S.FORM: nukai iname ia tia kovangvangʉn ma tapuroo sua ngʉco pusiam.
S.PHON: nukai iname ia tia kovaŋvaŋɨn ma tapur~ɾoo sua ŋɨʦo pusiam.
S.TRANSL: 若不驅趕過鳥,稻米就只有被麻雀吃光光.
If we did not drive them away, all the rice would be eaten out.

S.FORM: moncu nia mamarurang minʉna makasi na noo aratanavikarʉci a pusiam.
S.PHON: monʦu nia mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ minɨna makasi na noo ar~ɾatanavikar~ɾɨʦi a pusiam.
S.FORM: moncu nia mamarurang minʉna makasi na noo aratanavikarʉci a pusiam.
S.PHON: monʦu nia mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ minɨna makasi na noo ar~ɾatanavikar~ɾɨʦi a pusiam.
S.TRANSL: 老人家在那邊驅趕鳥,一直到稻田變成黃色的時候.
The elders drove birds away there until the color of the fields turned yellow.

S.FORM: noo tani tani tanavikarʉci pusiam ia tecu paripusiamʉn.
S.PHON: noo tani tani tanavikar~ɾɨʦi pusiam ia teʦu par~ɾipusiamɨn.
S.FORM: noo tani tani tanavikarʉci pusiam ia tecu paripusiamʉn.
S.PHON: noo tani tani tanavikar~ɾɨʦi pusiam ia teʦu par~ɾipusiamɨn.

TRANSL.notes: 當稻田變成黃色的時候, 要去使(稻米)被收割.

S.TRANSL: 當稻田變成黃色的時候, 要去使 被收割.
When the color of the fields turned yellow, it's time to harvest the rice.

S.FORM: nakai paripusiamʉn akia ta ngʉco tia tasʉ'a nia.
S.PHON: nakai par~ɾipusiamɨn akia ta ŋɨʦo tia tasɨʔa nia.
S.FORM: nakai paripusiamʉn akia ta ngʉco tia tasʉ'a nia.
S.PHON: nakai par~ɾipusiamɨn akia ta ŋɨʦo tia tasɨʔa nia.
S.TRANSL: 但是,收割稻米之後,卻沒有存放它們的場所.
However, having harvested them, we did not have the places to store.

S.FORM: tavarʉ' pacʉpʉcʉpʉng mamarurang a te ta pakʉnnanʉn una pusiam akia a tasʉ'a nia ta.
S.PHON: tavar~ɾɨʔ paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ a te ta pakɨnnanɨn una pusiam akia a tasɨʔa nia ta.
S.FORM: tavarʉ' pacʉpʉcʉpʉng mamarurang a te ta pakʉnnanʉn una pusiam akia a tasʉ'a nia ta.
S.PHON: tavar~ɾɨʔ paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ a te ta pakɨnnanɨn una pusiam akia a tasɨʔa nia ta.
S.TRANSL: 有稻米,卻沒有存放它們的場所.老人家會去想要怎麼辦.
Despite having no place to store the rice, elders would make a way out.

S.FORM: tia pakʉnnanʉn ngʉco umiriki ngʉco pusiam sua mamarurang miana ia tavarʉ'ʉn.
S.PHON: tia pakɨnnanɨn ŋɨʦo umir~ɾiki ŋɨʦo pusiam sua mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ miana ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨn.
S.FORM: tia pakʉnnanʉn ngʉco umiriki ngʉco pusiam sua mamarurang miana ia tavarʉ'ʉn.
S.PHON: tia pakɨnnanɨn ŋɨʦo umir~ɾiki ŋɨʦo pusiam sua mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ miana ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨn.
S.TRANSL: 要怎樣把稻米收堆起來,以前的老人家是知道的.
elders in the past knew how to gather the rice.

S.FORM: una ngʉco niarʉra kamanʉngkee satu'u kamanʉngkee satu'u kamanʉngkee ngʉco tarʉ.
S.PHON: una ŋɨʦo niar~ɾɨr~ɾa kamanɨŋkee satuʔu kamanɨŋkee satuʔu kamanɨŋkee ŋɨʦo tar~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: una ngʉco niarʉra kamanʉngkee satu'u kamanʉngkee satu'u kamanʉngkee ngʉco tarʉ.
S.PHON: una ŋɨʦo niar~ɾɨr~ɾa kamanɨŋkee satuʔu kamanɨŋkee satuʔu kamanɨŋkee ŋɨʦo tar~ɾɨ.

TRANSL.notes: 那裏有被鋸過的(木材),他們用以製成木箱,製作成床.

S.TRANSL: 那裏有被鋸過的 ,他們用以製成木箱,製作成床.

TRANSL.notes: There were what have been sawed. They used those (sawed woods) to make wooden boxes and beds.

S.TRANSL: There were what have been sawed. They used those to make wooden boxes and beds.

S.FORM: moca nia marivuraʉ marivuraʉ sua ngʉco pusiam ia mataparipari'i na satu'u makasuacu turupangci ngʉco maripusiam ia mo avicincu na tapininga pari.
S.PHON: moʦa nia mar~ɾivur~ɾaɨ mar~ɾivur~ɾaɨ sua ŋɨʦo pusiam ia matapar~ɾipar~ɾiʔi na satuʔu makasuaʦu tur~ɾupaŋʦi ŋɨʦo mar~ɾipusiam ia mo aviʦinʦu na tapiniŋa par~ɾi.
S.FORM: moca nia marivuraʉ marivuraʉ sua ngʉco pusiam ia mataparipari'i na satu'u makasuacu turupangci ngʉco maripusiam ia mo avicincu na tapininga pari.
S.PHON: moʦa nia mar~ɾivur~ɾaɨ mar~ɾivur~ɾaɨ sua ŋɨʦo pusiam ia matapar~ɾipar~ɾiʔi na satuʔu makasuaʦu tur~ɾupaŋʦi ŋɨʦo mar~ɾipusiam ia mo aviʦinʦu na tapiniŋa par~ɾi.

TRANSL.notes: 就那樣,稻米收割完之後,就帶著(稻米)到廣場曝曬.

S.TRANSL: 就那樣,稻米收割完之後,就帶著 到廣場曝曬.
Then, after reaping the rice, we dried them in the yard.

S.FORM: nuu parinci ia nuu maringunaaci ia m m una ngʉco pangi una ngʉco tarʉkʉra moncu nia mioru nukai tassa ia taturu.
S.PHON: nuu par~ɾinʦi ia nuu mar~ɾiŋunaaʦi ia m m una ŋɨʦo paŋi una ŋɨʦo tar~ɾɨkɨr~ɾa monʦu nia mior~ɾu nukai tassa ia tatur~ɾu.
S.FORM: nuu parinci ia nuu maringunaaci ia m m una ngʉco pangi una ngʉco tarʉkʉra moncu nia mioru nukai tassa ia taturu.
S.PHON: nuu par~ɾinʦi ia nuu mar~ɾiŋunaaʦi ia m m una ŋɨʦo paŋi una ŋɨʦo tar~ɾɨkɨr~ɾa monʦu nia mior~ɾu nukai tassa ia tatur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 在曝曬過之後,那邊有杵和臼,兩位到三位會在那邊舂搗.
After drying them, there were pestle and mortar. Two or three people would pound the rice there.

S.FORM: ioruuncukee makasi na akia tʉʉ.
S.PHON: ior~ɾuunʦukee makasi na akia tɨɨ.
S.FORM: ioruuncukee makasi na akia tʉʉ.
S.PHON: ior~ɾuunʦukee makasi na akia tɨɨ.
S.TRANSL: 他們會舂稻穀直到沒有稻殼.
They would pound until husks disappeared.

S.FORM: nuu makasuaci ia patakoruuncu vura ihua umʉsʉ'ʉ na 'uia.
S.PHON: nuu makasuaʦi ia patakor~ɾuunʦu vur~ɾa ihua umɨsɨʔɨ na ʔuia.
S.FORM: nuu makasuaci ia patakoruuncu vura ihua umʉsʉ'ʉ na 'uia.
S.PHON: nuu makasuaʦi ia patakor~ɾuunʦu vur~ɾa ihua umɨsɨʔɨ na ʔuia.
S.TRANSL: 那樣之後,就把那個米撈出來,放在鐵製的容器.
After that, rice will be scooped out and placed in iron containers.

S.FORM: siminsin po'ocipi.
S.PHON: siminsin poʔoʦipi.
S.FORM: siminsin po'ocipi.
S.PHON: siminsin poʔoʦipi.
S.TRANSL: 會被拿去沖洗和蒸煮.
the rice will be washed and cooked.

S.FORM: nuu macuru ci akia camai.
S.PHON: nuu maʦur~ɾu ʦi akia ʦamai.
S.FORM: nuu macuru ci akia camai.
S.PHON: nuu maʦur~ɾu ʦi akia ʦamai.
S.TRANSL: 當煮熟之後,沒有菜餚.
The rice was cooked, but there was no dish.

S.FORM: nakai sua mamarurang miana ia tavarʉ'ʉ una kunamʉ una apasʉ una arasakai.
S.PHON: nakai sua mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ miana ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨ una kunamɨ una apasɨ una ar~ɾasakai.
S.FORM: nakai sua mamarurang miana ia tavarʉ'ʉ una kunamʉ una apasʉ una arasakai.
S.PHON: nakai sua mamar~ɾur~ɾaŋ miana ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨ una kunamɨ una apasɨ una ar~ɾasakai.
S.TRANSL: 以前的老人家知道有川苔、螃蟹、蝦子.
Elders in the past knew there were moss, crabs and shrimps.

S.FORM: ihua ia camai miana ngʉco ihua ngʉco sua tia siakʉn ngʉco uru.
S.PHON: ihua ia ʦamai miana ŋɨʦo ihua ŋɨʦo sua tia siakɨn ŋɨʦo ur~ɾu.
S.FORM: ihua ia camai miana ngʉco ihua ngʉco sua tia siakʉn ngʉco uru.
S.PHON: ihua ia ʦamai miana ŋɨʦo ihua ŋɨʦo sua tia siakɨn ŋɨʦo ur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 這些川苔螃蟹蝦子就用來配飯.
These moss, crabs, and shrimps would be taken to be garnishes.
